title: MOSAIC: Multi-source Ocean Sensor and Model Integration Catalogue
description:
MOSAIC is an open-source Python framework for declarative, reproducible, and provenance-aware integration of heterogeneous oceanographic data, including satellite observations, reanalysis products, numerical-model outputs, and in-situ-compatible gridded datasets.

The framework provides YAML-defined workflows validated with Pydantic, multi-source ingestion, tiered semantic harmonisation, rule-based quality control, sandboxed derived-variable expressions, Zarr or NetCDF-CF export, and STAC provenance sidecars with BLAKE3-based pipeline and content hashes.

This release includes executable workflows, deterministic fixture data, tests, notebooks, CF and domain dictionaries, dependency manifests, and example provenance records. The distributed examples cover: (i) a Gulf of Riga coastal-upwelling workflow using Copernicus Marine SST and ERA5 wind data; (ii) a controlled Gulf of Mexico hurricane workflow demonstration; and (iii) a controlled Arctic sea-ice workflow demonstration.

The repository accompanies the manuscript MOSAIC -- An Open Python Framework for Reproducible Multi-Source Ocean Data Integration: A Gulf of Riga Case Study and Two Cross-Regime Workflow Demonstrations, prepared for prepared for Computers & Geosciences.

notes: This release is intended to support reproducible execution of the distributed fixture workflows without external credentials. Live-data workflows may require valid Copernicus Marine and Climate Data Store credentials and remain subject to upstream provider terms of use.
